Rationale: Fertility rates are closely tied to growth rates for an area and can be an excellent indicator of future population growth or decline in that area
Definition: Birth rate per 1,000 females aged 15 to 44 years
Data source: OHID, based on Office for National Statistics data
Indicator source: Office for Health Improvement and Disparities (OHID) based on Office for National Statistics births and population estimate data
Definition of numerator: Live births occurring in the respective calendar year.
Source of numerator: Office for National Statistics (ONS), Live births
Definition of denominator: Mid-year female population estimates (aged 15-44) for the respective calendar year.
Source of denominator: Office for National Statistics (ONS), Mid-year population estimates
Methodology: Live births in the respective calendar year divided by female 15-44 population. Records are assigned to a local area based on residence.
Disclosure control: 2010 onwards small numbers (<3) are suppressed where necessary. The counts and denominators for the Isles of Scilly have been combined with Cornwall, andcounts and denominators for City of London have been combined with Hackney.
Notes: Data was refreshed for 2022 based on the ONS revisions (July 2025) which were made to mid-2022 and mid-2023 population estimates to incorporate improved international and internal migration data.
